showings.title: The Soundman
showings.summary: “The Soundman” is a moving love story about passion, imagination, and the pursuit of humanity during dark times. Set against the marvelous world of 1940s Belgian radio, their voices rise above the rumble of…

showings.title: Eleanor the Great
showings.summary: “Eleanor the Great” features a bravura performance from June Squibb in the title role of the spirited 94-year-old who tells a tale that takes on a dangerous life of its own.

showings.title: Elena’s War
showings.summary: A stirring portrait of rebellion, resilience, and the cost of speaking truth to power, “Elena’s War” celebrates the indomitable spirit of a woman who refused to remain silent and dared to stand alone against tyranny…

showings.title: Midas Man
showings.summary: “Midas Man” tells the true story of the “Fifth Beatle” — The Beatles’ legendary manager Brian Epstein (in a star-making turn by Jacob Fortune-Lloyd, co-starring Emily Watson, Eddie Marsan, Eddie Izzard and Jay Leno.

showings.title: The Isolate Thief
showings.summary: One bitter-cold winter during the Civil War, a young woman becomes the lone caretaker of a remote Union Army outpost. When a stash of stolen gold falls into her lap, a gang of vicious outlaws comes looking for it.

showings.title: Outdoor Film: Blazing Saddles
showings.venue: Village of Oak Creek Association Park
showings.summary: “Blazing Saddles” (1974) — nominated for three Academy Awards — is a satirical comedy and Western spoof directed by Mel Brooks and starring Gene Wilder, Cleavon Little, Madeline Kahn and Harvey Korman.

showings.summary: Facing the death of his country’s glaciers and the loss of his beloved grandparents, Icelandic writer Andri Snær Magnason turns his archives into a time capsule to hold what is slipping away — family, memory, time…

showings.title: For the Love of a Woman
showings.summary: Search for others … find yourself. “For the Love of a Woman” is a sweeping English-language saga based on the beloved novel of Israeli author Meir Shalev, The Loves of Judith.

showings.title: Lucky Strike
showings.summary: Inspired by true events, “Lucky Strike” tells the story of a wounded American soldier trapped behind enemy lines during the last major German offensive during WWII, what will become known as The Battle of the Bulge.

showings.title: Outdoor Film: Rain Man
showings.venue: Village of Oak Creek Association Park
showings.summary: “Rain Man” (1988) was the winner of four Academy Awards including Best Picture, Best Director (Barry Levinson), Best Actor (Dustin Hoffman) and Best Screenplay. It was nominated for four other Oscars as well.

showings.title: Outdoor Film: An Officer and a Gentleman
showings.summary: “An Officer and a Gentleman” (1982) was nominated for six Academy Awards, winning two Oscars for Best Supporting Actor (Louis Gossett Jr.) and Best Original Song (Up Where We Belong).

showings.title: Gun Fury
showings.summary: Starring Rock Hudson and Donna Reed, “Gun Fury” follows Ben Warren (Hudson) who tracks down outlaw gang leader Frank Slayton (Philip Carey) after Slayton shot him and kidnapped his fiancée, Jennifer Ballard (Reed).

showings.title: WHAM! 10 Days in China
showings.summary: In 1985, WHAM! crossed cultural divides as the first Western pop act in China. Previously unseen, beautifully restored archive footage reveals this landmark meeting of pop, politics and history.

showings.summary: Psychic Standup is a show unlike any other. Karen Rontowski combines her 35 years as a standup comic with her 25 years of tarot reading and the results are predictably hilarious.
